name: Cuencas Mineras and Camino Olvidado Loop
name_original: Los Caminos de la Trashumancia – circular desde Villayuste (Reserva de la Biosfera Valles de Omaña y Luna)
description: Hike the moderate 9.7-mile Cuencas Mineras and Camino Olvidado Loop, gaining 1175 feet through varied mountain landscapes and historic areas
region_intro: Hiking around Soto Y Amío offers access to a diverse landscape within the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve of Omaña and Luna Valleys. The region features mountainous terrain with abundant oak forests, alongside lush plains and valleys carved by the Luna River. Notable geological formations like the Desfiladero de Los Calderones provide varied backdrops for outdoor activities. This area is characterized by a network of historical paths, now serving as hiking trails. Best hiking trails around Soto Y Amío The most popular hiking route is Los Calderones de Piedrasecha, an easy 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete, leading through impressive geological formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Cordel de Merinas Loop from Abelgas de Luna, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path exploring historic drover's roads and scenic gorges. Local hikers also love the Tierra de Ordás Trail, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail leading through diverse forest landscapes and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Soto Y Amío is defined by mountainous terrain, oak forests, and river valleys, with routes traversing ancient paths and geological features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Soto Y Amío are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 170 hikers have used komoot to explore Soto Y Amío's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Soto Y Amío?
A: Soto Y Amío offers a network of over 15 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 4 easy, 10 moderate, and 3 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for almost every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Soto Y Amío?
A: The terrain in Soto Y Amío is incredibly diverse, reflecting its status as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes, dense oak forests, and lush river valleys carved by the Luna River. The region also features unique geological formations, such as the impressive Desfiladero de Los Calderones, which add a dramatic element to many trails.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in Soto Y Amío?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous experience, there are 3 difficult routes available. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051369702/sierra-de-la-filera-peaks-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Filera Peaks Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 16 km and involves significant elevation gain, offering rewarding views for experienced hikers.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Soto Y Amío?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050859432/las-miedolas-circular-from-las-omanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Miédolas – circular from Las Omañas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.2 km loop that allows you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: What are some natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Soto Y Amío is rich in natural beauty and historical points of interest. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, discover the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;, or witness the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts several peaks, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fontañán Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in the area?
A: Yes, Soto Y Amío has 4 easy trails that are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041085482/los-calderones-de-piedrasecha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones de Piedrasecha&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is an easy 6 km path that takes less than two hours to complete, making it a great choice for a family outing to explore impressive geological formations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Soto Y Amío?
A: The best time to hike in Soto Y Amío is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Spring brings lush greenery and wildflowers, while autumn offers stunning fall foliage in the oak forests. Summers can be warm, and winters may present snow in higher elevations, making some trails more challenging.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Soto Y Amío?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Soto Y Amío, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain and oak forests to river valleys, and the well-preserved historical paths that now serve as scenic trails. The unique geological features like Los Calderones are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any routes that follow historical paths?
A: Yes, the region is known for its extensive network of paths historically used by locals and livestock. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051376209/el-cordel-de-merinas-loop-from-abelgas-de-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cordel de Merinas Loop from Abelgas de Luna&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.1 km route that specifically explores these historic drover's roads and scenic gorges, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting during hikes?
A: As part of the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve of Omaña and Luna Valleys, Soto Y Amío is rich in biodiversity. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, hikers often have the chance to spot various bird species, and if lucky, some of the local mammals that inhabit the oak forests and riverine environments. Maintaining quiet and observing from a distance increases your chances.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, many popular hiking areas and villages in Soto Y Amío offer designated parking spaces. It is advisable to check the details for your chosen route on komoot or local signage upon arrival to ensure convenient access.
tips: We are in the Alto del Camparón (passage area of the Los Caminos de la Transhumancia route) with beautiful views of the Omañeses mountains and to the north, the Luna mountain.
From Alto 'El Viso', we have a magnificent panoramic view of the Omaña mountains dominated by large expanses of oak groves.

Without a doubt, the king of the Oman mountains is the oak and here we find ourselves under the shade of a beautiful oak grove. A transit area of the route of Los Caminos de la Transhumancia that starts from Villayuste.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this hiking loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as moderate. While the full Camino Olvidado is considered a route of significant difficulty with continuous slopes and scarce infrastructure, this specific 15.7 km loop has a manageable elevation gain of about 358 meters, making it accessible for hikers with a reasonable fitness level. However, be prepared for varied terrain, including some potentially rocky or uneven sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain and scenery. You'll encounter mountain paths, open spaces, and dense pine forests. Expect continuous slopes throughout. The landscape features tranquil valleys, sections alongside streams, and views of historical and agricultural areas. You might also pass through areas with historical mining activity, reflecting the 'Cuencas Mineras' aspect.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop features several points of interest. You can enjoy panoramic views from Alto del Camparón and the View from Alto El Viso. The route also includes a section known as the Path of Fraternity.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other established trails. Approximately 28% of the loop follows parts of the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, and about 24% coincides with the Camino Olvidado (La Robla-Cirujales), which is a historical pilgrimage route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this loop is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months (May to October). During these periods, the weather is typically more favorable, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, as mountain conditions can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Is the Cuencas Mineras and Camino Olvidado Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given that it passes through natural areas and potentially near livestock, it's essential to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect wildlife and other hikers. Always pack out any waste.

faqs.question: Where can I park, and how do I access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The loop starts from Villayuste, within the Reserva de la Biosfera Valles de Omaña y Luna. Parking infrastructure may be limited due to the trail's less developed nature. It's advisable to look for available roadside parking in or near Villayuste, ensuring you do not obstruct local access or private property. Public transport options to this specific starting point are likely scarce, so driving is usually the most practical way to reach the trailhead.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the biosphere reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this loop or to access the Reserva de la Biosfera Valles de Omaña y Luna. However, always adhere to any local regulations or signage you may encounter, especially regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, you should bring sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers for varying weather conditions. Given the varied terrain and less abundant signage, a reliable navigation tool (like the komoot app with the downloaded tour) is highly recommended. Sun protection, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone are also essential.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for families with children or beginner hikers?
faqs.answer: While rated moderate, the continuous slopes and varied terrain, including potentially rocky sections, mean it might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. Families with older, experienced children or beginners with a good level of fitness should be fine. It's not recommended for individuals with reduced mobility.

faqs.question: What makes the 'Camino Olvidado' part of this route unique?
faqs.answer: The 'Camino Olvidado' (Forgotten Way) is one of the oldest documented pilgrimage routes of the Camino de Santiago, used between the 9th and 12th centuries. It's known for being less trodden and more tranquil than other Camino routes, offering a unique historical and cultural experience away from the crowds. Its name reflects its decline in use after the Reconquest, though efforts are now underway to recover and improve it.
